content_decay.issue: Outdated news article — December 2025 deadline has passed
content_decay.details: Article written September 2025 frames December 16, 2025 as future event. Uses forward-looking language that is now stale. Reader in 2026 finds guidance no longer applicable.
content_decay.fix: Add 'Update' banner at top noting outcome, link to latest ban status article, or noindex/archive

content_decay.details: dateModified same as published. The TikTok ban situation has evolved significantly since September 2025.

content_decay.fix: Expand to 2,500+ words with timeline data, add quarterly update schedule

content_decay.issue: Thin content — only ~1,900 words for high-competition keyword
content_decay.details: Competitors rank with 3,000-5,000 word articles including detailed charts. Missing niche-specific timing data.
content_decay.fix: Expand to 3,000+ words, add visual posting schedules

content_decay.issue: Thin data article — ~1,900 words with limited granularity
content_decay.details: Data articles need more detailed tables, multiple view count tiers, and monthly update cadence to stay competitive.

content_decay.issue: No 'last updated' date visible to users
content_decay.details: Calculator pages like /calculators/tiktok-money/, /calculators/engagement-rate/, /calculators/coins/, /calculators/brand-deal-rate/ show no visible publication or update date. Users cannot verify data freshness.
content_decay.fix: Add visible 'Last updated: [date]' below calculator heading

content_decay.details: Function in src/lib/content/article-page.tsx prepends primaryKeyword in lowercase when not found in title, creating patterns like 'best time to post tiktok - Best Posting Times for TikTok by'. Affects all MDX-generated articles where primaryKeyword differs from title wording. Creates duplicate keyword appearance that looks spammy to Google and users.
content_decay.fix: Rewrite normalizeSeoTitle() to either: (a) use the title as-is when it contains the topic naturally, or (b) append keyword naturally rather than prepending in lowercase
